annotations.label: Gödel numbering

anchor.snippet: a Gödel numbering is a function that assigns to each symbol and well-formed formula

annotations.note: Mathlib has a Gödel numbering for partial recursive function codes (file header literally titled "Gödel Numbering for Partial Recursive Functions") but not a general definition for arbitrary formal-language symbols and formulas.

annotations.label: Gödel's prime-power encoding of sequences

anchor.snippet: the Gödel encoding of the sequence is the product of the first n primes raised to their corresponding values in the sequence

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes the alternative Chinese-remainder-based encoding (`Nat.beta`) rather than the prime-power product encoding ∏ pᵢ^{aᵢ}.

annotations.label: Unique recovery via fundamental theorem of arithmetic

anchor.snippet: any number (and, in particular, a number obtained in this way) can be uniquely factored into prime factors

annotations.note: Unique prime factorization on ℕ is fully formalized; `Nat.factorization_prod_pow_eq_self` recovers a natural number from its prime exponents.

annotations.label: Gödel number of "0 = 0" (Nagel and Newman)

anchor.snippet: the Gödel number of the formula

annotations.note: This is a specific worked numerical example of Nagel–Newman's scheme and has no counterpart in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Non-uniqueness of Gödel numberings

anchor.snippet: Infinitely many different Gödel numberings are possible

annotations.note: Mathlib does not state non-uniqueness of Gödel numberings as a theorem (no formal `Gödel numbering` predicate is defined).

annotations.label: Alternative Gödel numbering via bijective base-K

anchor.snippet: an alternative Gödel numbering could be constructed by invertibly mapping this set of symbols

annotations.note: No bijective-base-K encoding of symbol strings is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Course-of-values recursion is primitive recursive

anchor.snippet: show how functions defined by course-of-values recursion are in fact primitive recursive functions

annotations.note: `Primrec.nat_strong_rec` shows strong (course-of-values) recursion with a primitive recursive step is itself primitive recursive.

annotations.label: Inference rules as arithmetical functions

anchor.section: Expressing statements and proofs by numbers
anchor.snippet: each inference rule of the theory can be expressed as a function on the natural numbers

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize an arithmetization of a deductive system's inference rules (the lean4-logic project does this separately, see `GodelBetaFunction` references).

annotations.label: Generalized Gödel numbering in computability theory

anchor.snippet: Any assignment of the elements of a formal language to natural numbers in such a way that the numbers can be manipulated by an algorithm

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Encodable`/`Denumerable` and the explicit `Denumerable` instance on `Nat.Partrec.Code` realize this generalized algorithmic numbering, but no abstract `IsGödelNumbering` predicate is defined.

annotations.label: Gödel sets

anchor.snippet: Gödel sets are sometimes used in set theory to encode formulas

annotations.note: No set-theoretic Gödel-set encoding of formulas is present in Mathlib.
